title: vangueria rufescens subsp. rufescens
intro: Vangueria rufescens subsp. rufescens, also known as the bitter bush or African bitter apple, is a plant species native to Africa. This shrub-like plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Vangueria rufescens subsp. rufescens include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Vangueria rufescens subsp. rufescens include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
